title: Quiz: Crosstalk Characterization
description: Test your understanding of crosstalk sources, measurement, and mitigation

questions.question: What is ZZ coupling in superconducting qubits?

options.text: Always-on interaction causing state-dependent frequency shifts
Coupling between qubit and readout resonator

questions.explanation: ZZ coupling is a residual always-on interaction between coupled transmon qubits. It arises from capacitive coupling and causes state-dependent frequency shifts: the frequency of one qubit depends on whether neighboring qubits are in |0> or |1>. This leads to unwanted phase accumulation during idle periods.

questions.question: How is ZZ coupling typically measured?

options.text: T1 measurement with varying neighbor states
Conditional Ramsey experiment comparing frequencies with neighbor in |0> vs |1>

questions.explanation: ZZ coupling is measured using conditional Ramsey experiments. You run Ramsey on one qubit while preparing the neighboring qubit in either |0> or |1>. The difference in observed Ramsey frequencies gives the ZZ coupling strength directly.

questions.question: What does simultaneous RB reveal that individual RB cannot?

options.text: Additional errors that occur only when multiple qubits are driven together

questions.explanation: Simultaneous RB detects crosstalk-induced errors that only appear when multiple qubits are operated at the same time. By comparing simultaneous EPC to individual EPC, you can quantify the additional error from crosstalk during parallel operations.

questions.question: What is addressability error?

options.text: Error in reading out the qubit state
Unintended rotation of a spectator qubit when driving a target qubit
Error in the qubit frequency calibration
Memory addressing error in the control computer

questions.explanation: Addressability error quantifies how well you can control one qubit without affecting others. When you apply a gate to a target qubit, spectator qubits should remain unchanged. Any unintended rotation of spectators is an addressability error.

questions.question: How can ZZ coupling be mitigated during idle periods?

options.text: By using echo pulses to refocus the accumulated phase

questions.explanation: Echo pulses (like spin echo or dynamical decoupling) can refocus the phase accumulated due to ZZ coupling. By inserting X or Y pulses at appropriate times, the ZZ-induced phase can be cancelled, similar to how echo cancels dephasing from frequency fluctuations.

questions.question: Frequency crowding becomes less problematic as the number of qubits increases.
questions.explanation: False. Frequency crowding becomes MORE problematic as qubit count increases. With more qubits sharing limited spectral bandwidth, the probability of frequency collisions increases. Careful frequency planning and techniques like tunable couplers become essential at scale.
